one_time_description: Agricultural Benefits Specific Claim settlement with Canada awarded $41,764,865 (settled June 2018, trust established; income from trust reflected in current year)
economic_notes: Corporate restructuring during year transferred operations to newly formed limited partnerships. First Nation has loan guarantees for related parties totalling $6,733,539. COVID-19 pandemic declared March 11, 2020 with potential future impacts noted. Multiple business trusts and limited partnerships operating in oilfield services, camp and catering, real estate, and industry relations sectors.
notes: Audit opinion is qualified due to inability to determine impairment of investments held by Chipewyan Prairie Business Trust. Three entities fully consolidated (Land Claim Settlement Trust, Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust, Community Trust). Fifteen entities accounted for by modified equity method. Prior year revenue of $50.6M was significantly higher due to the $41.7M Agricultural Benefits Settlement Trust funding flowing through in FY2019. ISC funding dropped dramatically from $44.8M in 2019 to $4.4M in 2020 likely because the settlement was recorded in 2019. total_government_transfers includes ISC $4,440,505 + CMHC $267,814 + ATC $131,678 + Other government funding $97,503 + FNDF $207,026 = $5,144,526 (approximate). annual_debt_service represents net repayment on long-term debt per cash flow statement.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
